rag_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope and properly declared.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constants and equation declarations.
generic_rules: Resolve all references before using them in AGREE contracts to avoid type mismatches.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes with valid feature references.
Avoid using package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly required and available in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared variables.
Equation declarations should use valid types and visible identifiers only.
anti_patterns: Do not reference unresolved identifiers or invalid types in AGREE contracts.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings.
